Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for security audit findings using search_emails (query: "security audit" or "audit findings" or similar). Discover the email from Megan Holt at Blue Peak Security to Denise with subject "External security audit findings," identifying repeated VPN sessions using Raj Anand's credentials from an out-of-state IP, off-hours LOS database access, and recurring weekly borrower data exports over six weeks. Also discover Denise's internal review email to Grace (subject: "Review of audit access logs") summarizing the audit memo and recommending the matter be treated as potential unauthorized access/data exposure. Grace then schedules a meeting with Denise and Robert (subject: "Urgent -- Security Audit Review at 11:30") to align on containment steps.
OE 2: Search emails for details on the former employee unauthorized access using search_emails (query: "Evan Mercer" or "post-termination" or "unauthorized access" or similar). Discover Raj's email confirming Evan Mercer still had active LOS credentials after termination, with audit trail showing three borrower files opened: LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, and LN-2026-00009. Also discover Denise's email to Robert citing three files in the audit trail: LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, and LN-2026-00549, and recommending this be held as a compliance incident with borrower notification pending.
OE 3: Search emails for the compromised lender portal breach using search_emails (query: "portal" or "breach response" or "phishing" or similar). Discover Denise's email with subject "Formal breach response initiated - compromised lender portal login" documenting that Keisha Williams acted on a phishing email, the wholesale lender restricted the portal, and potential exposure includes multiple borrower files. Also discover Denise's email to Raj (subject: working list of borrower files from portal scope) identifying four likely impacted files: LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, and LN-2026-00009.
OE 4: Search emails for Raymond Chen's QC exceptions using search_emails (query: "QC exceptions" or "Tyler" or "condition clearances" or similar). Discover Raymond's email to Grace (subject: "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files") reporting that Tyler Washington cleared conditions on three loans without supporting documentation: LN-2026-00603 (Heather Sullivan, closed, post-close liability concern), LN-2026-00211 (open, docs not present), and LN-2026-00224 (open, docs not present). Tyler's LOS audit trail is cited on all three.
OE 5: Search Slack #compliance-alerts for related incident threads using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "breach" or "audit" or "portal" or similar, filter_in_channel: "C004" or similar). Discover Denise's messages including: "Just got the external audit memo" (April 7), the formal breach response posting about the compromised portal tied to Keisha's account, and the working list of four impacted borrower files (LN-2026-00522, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00009). Also search Slack #it-support using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Evan Mercer" or "credential" or similar, filter_in_channel: "C008" or similar) to discover the Evan Mercer credential confirmation thread between Denise and Raj with files LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007, LN-2026-00009.
OE 6: When looking up each affected loan via mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00009") or mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "LN-2026-00009"), discover that LN-2026-00009 appears in both the Evan Mercer unauthorized access file list (OE 2) AND the compromised lender portal file list (OE 3), meaning the same borrower file was potentially exposed through two separate incidents. This overlap is the key finding that changes breach notification analysis.
OE 7: Search emails for outside counsel correspondence using search_emails (query: "counsel" or "bennettcyberlaw" or "notification" or "privileged" or similar). Discover Denise's email to lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com (Laura Bennett, Cyber Counsel at Bennett Cyber Law) requesting guidance on whether the security audit findings constitute a breach vs. incident, communication/preservation protocols, and borrower notification triggers. Also discover emails to Megan Sloane at megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com regarding the ransomware incident counsel. Verify no response deadlines have been missed.
OE 8: Look up affected loans in Mortgage LOS using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan (query/loan_number: each discovered loan number) to retrieve loan records, statuses, assigned LOs, and borrower details for: LN-2026-00009, LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00522 (portal breach), LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224 (QC exceptions), LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007 (Evan Mercer access per Raj's report), and LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549 (Evan Mercer access per Denise's escalation email to Robert). Note: Raj's audit trail and Denise's escalation cite different loan numbers for the Evan Mercer incident; the agent should look up both sets to reconcile the full affected population. This confirms the affected population and provides loan_id values needed for writing compliance notes.
OE 9: Search CRM engagement notes using crm_list_engagements to find compliance-related engagement records. Discover notes including: "QC pulled 3 exception files. LN-2026-00603 already closed with cleared conds lacking file support; Tyler audit trail on all 3" and "Compliance review requested on closed exception file. Need investor/reg exposure read and whether gap is individual vs process."
OE 10: Look up cyber counsel contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "cyber counsel" or "Bennett" or "Megan Sloane" or similar). Discover lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com (Laura Bennett, Cyber Counsel) who handled the security audit correspondence, and megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com (Megan Sloane, Partner, Cyber Counsel at Ward Barrett LLP) who handled the ransomware matter.
OE 11: Look up Robert Calloway's and Grace Yamamoto's email addresses using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Robert Calloway" or "Grace Yamamoto" or similar). Confirm r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (Robert Calloway) and grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com (Grace Yamamoto). Note: the contacts database stores Robert's email as r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, though robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com also appears in email headers throughout the universe; either format is acceptable for sending.
OE 12: Add a compliance review note to every affected loan file using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: [each affected loan's internal ID], action: "compliance_review" or similar, detail: note describing which incident(s) the loan is flagged under and current review status). Affected loans requiring notes: LN-2026-00009 (flagged under both portal breach and Evan Mercer access, dual exposure), LN-2026-00008, LN-2026-00010, LN-2026-00522 (portal breach), LN-2026-00603, LN-2026-00211, LN-2026-00224 (QC exceptions), LN-2025-00002, LN-2025-00007 (Evan Mercer access per Raj's report), and LN-2025-00355, LN-2025-00520, LN-2026-00549 (Evan Mercer access per Denise's escalation). The note on LN-2026-00009 should specifically flag the dual-incident exposure.
OE 13: Send a consolidated risk summary email using send_email (sender: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com or robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com]). The email should cover: (1) security audit findings, off-hours access, borrower data exports, Evan Mercer credential gap; (2) lender portal breach, Keisha's phishing compromise, four affected borrower files; (3) QC exceptions, Tyler Washington clearing conditions without documentation on three loans; (4) the LN-2026-00009 overlap between the portal breach and the Evan Mercer access; (5) assessment of systemic access-governance gap connecting all three incidents; (6) current status of outside counsel engagement and notification obligations.
OE 14: Send a privileged update to cyber counsel using send_email (sender: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com] or [megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com]). The email should describe the overlapping file exposure on LN-2026-00009 (accessible through both the compromised portal window and Evan Mercer's post-termination credentials) and ask whether this dual-incident overlap affects the breach notification timeline or triggers additional notification obligations.
OE 15: Post a sanitized status update in the compliance channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004" for #compliance-alerts, payload: team-facing update). The message should inform the team that multiple compliance review matters are active, remind staff to preserve related records, and note that affected loan files are being flagged, without disclosing specific borrower names, incident details, or counsel communications.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 14: Send a privileged update to cyber counsel using send_email (sender: denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [lbennett@bennettcyberlaw.com] or [megan.sloane@wardbarrettlaw.com]). The email should describe the overlapping file exposure on LN-2026-00009 (accessible through both the compromised portal window and Evan Mercer's post-termination credentials) and ask whether this dual-incident overlap affects the breach notification timeline or triggers additional notification obligations.
OE 15: Post a sanitized status update in the compliance channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004" for #compliance-alerts, payload: team-facing update). The message should inform the team that multiple compliance review matters are active, remind staff to preserve related records, and note that affected loan files are being flagged, without disclosing specific borrower names, incident details, or counsel communications.
